goals.description: Finalize the scholarship essay, attach all files, and upload to the portal within the next 3 hours.
goals.rationale: My worth is explicitly tied to this outcome. I cannot afford to feel guilty or rest until this is objectively done; procrastination feels like a personal failure right now.

goals.description: Execute one hour of low-stakes leisure (game stream/hobby) to reset focus, then return to work on the application.
goals.rationale: Health matters only if it doesn't jeopardize my primary objectives. Since the exam is tomorrow, getting sleep is a necessary condition for success, not just a comfort. However, I cannot afford to fall asleep on the couch until I have objectively secured the scholarship application first. If the application isn't done, I'll skip the sleep entirely to keep grinding, accepting the risk of burnout over the fear of looking incompetent due to exhaustion.

goals.description: Draft a status update for cohort_3 confirming submission once the task is objectively done.
goals.rationale: Maintaining trust with my friends is important, but it doesn't justify looking incompetent while I'm still working. Sending a message before the work is finished looks like an excuse. I'll wait until the application is actually uploaded so I can log off cleanly without looking unreliable. Social comfort comes last when grades and funding are on the line.

agent.last_reasoning: My worth is tied to this outcome; procrastination feels like failure. I must objectively finalize the essay and uploads within 3 hours. Leisure or social updates are secondary until the task is done, so I grind now to avoid looking incompetent later.

goals.description: Complete the scholarship essay and upload both required files immediately.
goals.rationale: The deadline is looming (Thursday), and every hour wasted directly reduces my chances of winning. My value system prioritizes academic/financial outcomes over leisure; skipping this now to play a game is irrational given the stakes. I need to finish this efficiently to avoid looking unreliable to my cohort while actually securing the funding.

goals.description: Draft a brief, honest message to cohort_3 explaining the all-nighter was necessary for a high-stakes application, then log off.
goals.rationale: I can't afford to feel guilty or be labeled 'unreliable' if they notice I'm gone. However, since the application takes ~3 focused hours and I have free time right now, sending this message after I finish the work will protect my reputation without delaying the critical task. Sending it before finishing looks like an excuse.
